title: Port renderer from React to SolidJS
description: Migrate the entire Electron renderer (~212 components, 56 hooks, 10 contexts) from React 18 to SolidJS. Use solid-ui.com for UI component replacements. Big-bang approach — React and Solid cannot coexist in the same render tree.
design:
Big-bang migration on the soolid branch. Previous attempt on feature/cuddly-tools-tickle-6za is read-only reference.

Order of operations:
1. Swap build toolchain (vite, tsconfig, deps)
2. Foundation layer (entry point, router, contexts, hooks) + UI layer (solid-ui.com components, icons)
3. Feature components in parallel batches
4. TypeScript fix-up and build verification

Key libraries:
- solid-ui.com (Kobalte-based shadcn port) for UI primitives
- @tanstack/solid-router and @tanstack/solid-query (near-identical API to React versions)
- @corvu/resizable for panel resizing
- cmdk-solid for command palette
- lucide-solid for icons

Framework-agnostic code (src/renderer/lib/, terminal/, constants/, types/, styles/) needs zero changes.
Main process and IPC layer are untouched.
acceptance_criteria:
- App builds without errors (bun run build)
- App launches in Electron dev mode
- All major features work: workspace, tasks, terminal, editor, settings, command palette, sidebar, integrations
- Zero React imports remain in src/renderer/
- No new tests required — manual verification only
notes:
Assumptions:
- solid-ui.com covers ~85% of shadcn components. Gaps: scroll-area (use native CSS), input/textarea (use TextField)
- User will verify everything manually
- Existing tests are deleted, not ported
- Branch soolid already has preparatory cleanup (removed in-app preview, feedback modal)

title: Swap build toolchain to SolidJS and remove test infrastructure
description: Replace React build dependencies and config with SolidJS equivalents so the renderer compiles under Solid's JSX transform.
design:
package.json changes:
- Remove: react, react-dom, @types/react, @types/react-dom, @vitejs/plugin-react, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, vitest, jsdom, all 17 @radix-ui/react-* packages (accordion, alert-dialog, checkbox, collapsible, context-menu, dialog, label, popover, progress, radio-group, scroll-area, select, separator, slot, switch, toast, tooltip), cmdk, react-resizable-panels
- Add: solid-js, vite-plugin-solid, @tanstack/solid-router, @tanstack/solid-query, @kobalte/core, @corvu/resizable, cmdk-solid, lucide-solid
- Keep: monaco-editor, @xterm/*, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, tailwindcss, dompurify, marked

vite.config.ts: Replace react() plugin with solid() from vite-plugin-solid. Change icons compiler jsx: react → jsx: solid. Remove vitest test project configs.
tsconfig.json: jsx: preserve, jsxImportSource: solid-js, remove react/react-dom types.
components.json: Update for solid-ui if needed.

Test cleanup:
- Delete src/test/ directory entirely
- Delete all *.test.ts and *.test.tsx files (~100 files)
- Remove test scripts from package.json

Reference: feature/cuddly-tools-tickle-6za branch has working vite.config.ts and tsconfig.json for Solid.
acceptance_criteria:
- bun install succeeds
- vite.config.ts uses vite-plugin-solid with no vitest config
- tsconfig.json has jsx: preserve, jsxImportSource: solid-js
- No React packages in dependencies
- No test files remain in src/
- No test-related devDependencies
notes:
Use bun for package management (user preference).
The previous attempt branch has tested config that can be referenced.

title: Convert diff viewer, Monaco, and file explorer
description: Convert diff visualization, Monaco editor wrappers, and file explorer/tree components.
design:
Files:
- diff-viewer/ (13 files): DiffViewer, DiffPanel, DiffToolbar, ChangesTab, HistoryTab, CommitArea, CommitFileList, FileDiffView, FileList, StackedDiffView, DiffWarnings, plus comments (CommentCard, CommentInput, CommentWidget)
- monaco/ (2 files): MonacoEditor.tsx, MonacoDiffEditor.tsx — thin wrappers around imperative DOM API. Convert useRef + useEffect to let ref + onMount/onCleanup.
- FileExplorer/ (8 files): FileTree, FileTabs, CodeEditor, EditorHeader, MarkdownPreview, SearchInput, FileIcons, ContentSearchResults
- EditorMode.tsx

Monaco is framework-agnostic at the DOM level — the wrappers just manage lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria:
- Diff viewer renders file diffs
- Monaco editor loads and is editable
- File explorer tree navigates files
- Code editor tabs work
- No React imports
notes: Monaco and diff components are mostly imperative DOM — straightforward conversion.

title: Convert project, task, and kanban components
description: Convert project management, task management, and kanban board components.
design:
Files:
- ~20 task/project components: ProjectMainView, ProjectDeleteButton, TaskItem, TaskList, TaskModal (47+ hooks), TaskHoverActionCard, TaskChanges, TaskAdvancedSettings, TaskSettingsCard, TaskTerminalPanel, TaskStatusIndicator, TaskContextBadges, TaskCreationLoading, etc.
- kanban/ (4 files): KanbanBoard, KanbanCard, KanbanColumn, AgentTooltip
- FileChangesPanel.tsx

TaskModal is the third most complex component (47 hooks).
Kanban uses drag-and-drop patterns that need Solid event handling.
acceptance_criteria:
- Task list renders and is interactive
- Task modal opens and saves
- Kanban board displays columns and cards
- Project views load correctly
- No React imports
notes: TaskModal complexity rivals Workspace and ChatInterface.

title: Convert sidebar, titlebar, and navigation components
description: Convert sidebar navigation, window titlebar, and command palette components.
design:
Files:
- sidebar/ (3 files): LeftSidebar and sub-components
- titlebar/ (8 files): Titlebar, WindowControls, PerformanceChip, TitlebarMenu, etc.
- CommandPalette.tsx — now uses cmdk-solid instead of cmdk
- CommandPaletteWrapper.tsx
- AppKeyboardShortcuts.tsx

Command palette: swap cmdk React imports to cmdk-solid. API should be similar.
Titlebar uses window.electronAPI for window controls — framework agnostic.
acceptance_criteria:
- Left sidebar renders and navigates
- Titlebar shows with window controls
- Command palette opens, searches, and executes commands
- Keyboard shortcuts work
- No React imports
notes: cmdk-solid is a native Solid port — API similar but not identical to cmdk React.

title: Convert integrations, SSH, MCP, skills, and automations
description: Convert all integration forms, SSH connection UI, MCP server UI, skills/agents view, and automations components.
design:
Files:
- integrations/ (7): GithubIssueSelector, GitLabIssueSelector, JiraIssueSelector, LinearIssueSelector, ForgejoIssueSelector, IntegrationRow, IntegrationsCard + tooltip previews
- ssh/ (7): SshSettingsCard, SshConnectionForm, SshConnectionList, SshConnectionTestButton, AddRemoteProjectModal, RemoteProjectIndicator, ConnectionStatusBadge
- mcp/ (3): McpPage and sub-components
- skills/ (4): SkillsView and sub-components
- automations/ (6): AutomationsView, AutomationRow, AutomationInlineCreate, AutomationRunningTasks, RunLogsModal

These are mostly form-heavy CRUD components. Mechanical conversion.
acceptance_criteria:
- All integration selectors render and fetch issues
- SSH connection forms work
- MCP server page loads
- Skills view displays
- Automations view with create/run/logs
- No React imports
notes: 27 files total but mostly straightforward form components.

title: Convert remaining miscellaneous components
description: Convert all remaining components not covered by other tasks: agent components, modals, git-related, PR components, and misc.
design:
~25 files including:
- Agent components: AgentSelector, AgentDropdown, AgentInfoCard, AgentLogo, AgentRow, AgentTooltipRow, MultiAgentDropdown, MultiAgentTask
- Modal components: CloneFromUrlModal, ConfigEditorModal, ExternalLinkModal, MoveChangesToTaskModal, NewProjectModal, ChangelogModal, DeletePrNotice, UpdateModal, CustomCommandModal
- Git/PR: BranchSelect, BaseBranchControls, PrReviewStatus, PrPreviewTooltip, OpenPrsSection, MergePrSection, DeleteRiskFileList, PrCommentsList, CheckRunsList
- Misc: RunLauncher, RunList, ReorderList, SidebarEmptyState, InstallBanner, UpdateCard, RequirementsNotice, CliAgentsList
- ModalRenderer.tsx (centralized modal rendering)

All follow standard conversion patterns.
acceptance_criteria:
- All agent dropdowns/selectors work
- All modals open and close correctly
- Branch select and PR components render
- ModalRenderer dispatches to correct modal
- No React imports
notes: Catch-all for everything not in other batches. Most are small, simple components.

title: Fix TypeScript errors and verify build
description: Run tsc and vite build to find and fix all remaining type errors and build issues. Verify app launches in Electron.
design:
Steps:
1. tsc --noEmit — fix all TypeScript errors systematically
2. vite build — fix all bundler errors
3. vite dev — verify app loads in Electron
4. Manual walkthrough checklist:
   - App launches, shows welcome/workspace
   - Navigate between projects
   - Open tasks, task modal
   - Terminal renders and accepts input
   - Monaco editor loads
   - Command palette opens
   - Sidebar toggles
   - File explorer works
   - Settings page renders
   - Integrations section loads

Common issues to watch for:
- Destructured props (breaks Solid reactivity)
- Missing Show/For wrappers
- Signal access without calling getter
- Stale refs from React patterns
acceptance_criteria:
- tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
- bun run build completes successfully
- App launches in Electron dev mode without crashes
- All major features render and are interactive
notes: This is the final gating task. All other tasks must complete first.

title: Delete test infrastructure
description: Remove all test files and test configuration. No tests will be ported — user will verify manually.
design:
- Delete src/test/ directory entirely
- Delete all *.test.ts and *.test.tsx files across the codebase
- Remove test config from vite.config.ts (the two vitest project configs)
- Remove @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, vitest, jsdom from devDependencies
- Remove any test scripts from package.json
acceptance_criteria:
- No test files remain in src/
- No test-related devDependencies
- vite.config.ts has no vitest configuration
notes: Can be done in parallel with the toolchain swap.

title: Convert entry point, router, and app shell
description: Convert the renderer bootstrap path from React to Solid: main.tsx, App.tsx, router.tsx, AppShell.tsx. This is the spine of the app — everything else depends on it.
design:
src/renderer/main.tsx: ReactDOM.createRoot().render() → render() from solid-js/web
src/renderer/App.tsx: @tanstack/react-router → @tanstack/solid-router (RouterProvider, etc.)
src/renderer/router.tsx: Same import swap. API nearly identical: createRouter, createRoute, createRootRoute, RouterProvider, Outlet all exist in solid-router. Hash history via createHashHistory().
src/renderer/AppShell.tsx: Convert provider nesting. useState → createSignal, useEffect → createEffect/onMount, {children} → {props.children}. This file nests 10+ context providers.

TanStack Solid Router mirrors the React API almost exactly. The route tree structure stays the same.
acceptance_criteria:
- render() from solid-js/web bootstraps the app
- Router creates hash-based routes matching existing route tree
- AppShell nests all converted context providers
- No React imports in these 4 files

title: Convert 10 context providers to Solid
description: Convert all React context providers to Solid's createContext + signals pattern. These provide app-wide state that every component depends on.
design:
Mechanical transform for each provider in src/renderer/contexts/:
- React.createContext → Solid createContext (same API shape)
- useState → createSignal
- useEffect → createEffect / onMount / onCleanup
- useCallback → plain function (not needed in Solid)
- {children} → {props.children}
- useQuery from @tanstack/react-query → createQuery from @tanstack/solid-query (wrap options in a function)

Files:
- AppContextProvider.tsx (uses useQuery)
- AppSettingsProvider.tsx
- EmdashAccountProvider.tsx
- GithubContextProvider.tsx
- ModalProvider.tsx
- ProjectManagementProvider.tsx
- TaskManagementContext.tsx
- ExternalLinkContext.tsx
- GitWorkspaceBusyContext.tsx
- KeyboardSettingsContext.tsx
acceptance_criteria:
- All 10 contexts export Solid-compatible createContext + Provider
- useContext consumption works from child components
- Query-based contexts use createQuery from @tanstack/solid-query
- No React imports in any context file
notes: Can run in parallel with entry point conversion once toolchain is ready.

title: Convert 56 custom hooks to Solid primitives
description: Convert all custom hooks in src/renderer/hooks/ from React hooks to Solid reactive primitives.
design:
Conversion patterns:
- useQuery({queryKey, queryFn}) → createQuery(() => ({queryKey, queryFn})) from @tanstack/solid-query
- useState → createSignal
- useMemo → createMemo
- useRef → let ref: T | undefined
- useCallback → drop wrapper entirely
- useEffect(..., [deps]) → createEffect(() => { /* deps auto-tracked */ onCleanup(...) })

Complexity tiers:
Trivial (~20): useLocalStorage, useTheme, useIsMonochrome, useFeatureFlag, useTelemetryConsent, useStatusUnread, useTaskUnread, useTaskBusy, useTaskStatus, useConversationStatus, useFooterBranch, usePanelLayout, useAgentPreference, useTaskSettings, useTaskAgentNames, useContentSearch, useTerminalSearch, useTerminalSelection, useOpenInApps

Medium (~25, involve useQuery/useMutation): useFileChanges, usePullRequests, useCheckRuns, useAutoCheckRunsRefresh, useAutoPrRefresh, useFileDiff, useFileIndex, usePrComments, usePrStatus, useCreatePR, useDeleteRisks, useRemoteProject, useProjectRemoteInfo, useSshConnections, useUpdateNotifier, useUpdater, useWorkspaceConnection

Complex (~11, DOM refs/events): useAgentEvents, useAutoScrollOnTaskSwitch, useCommentInjection, useEditorDiffDecorations, useDiffEditorComments, useLineComments, useKeyboardShortcuts, useTerminalViewportWheelForwarding, useWorkspaceRouter
acceptance_criteria:
- All 56 hooks converted to Solid-compatible functions
- createQuery used instead of useQuery for data fetching
- No React hook imports (useState, useEffect, etc.) remain
- Hooks that return signals follow Solid conventions (getter/setter tuple)
notes:
Some hooks depend on context values — ensure context providers are converted first or convert in parallel with stub types.
Solid hooks are just functions that call createSignal/createEffect — no rules-of-hooks restrictions.

title: Replace shadcn/ui components with solid-ui.com
description: Replace all 33 shadcn/ui components in src/renderer/components/ui/ with solid-ui.com equivalents built on Kobalte and Corvu.
design:
Use npx solidui-cli@latest add [name] or copy from solid-ui.com for:
accordion, alert, alert-dialog, badge, breadcrumb, button, card, checkbox, collapsible, context-menu, dialog, label, popover, progress, radio-group, resizable (Corvu-based), select, separator, sidebar, switch, toast, tooltip, command (cmdk-solid)

Gaps to handle manually:
- scroll-area → native CSS overflow-auto with custom scrollbar styling
- input → solid-ui TextField component
- textarea → solid-ui TextField textarea variant

Custom components to convert manually (mostly pure markup):
- markdown-renderer.tsx: swap dangerouslySetInnerHTML to innerHTML, uses marked + dompurify
- relative-time.tsx: tiny interval component
- spinner.tsx: pure SVG
- shortcut-hint.tsx: pure markup
- slug-input.tsx: simple input wrapper
- pr-status-skeleton.tsx: pure markup
- toaster.tsx: wraps toast
- right-sidebar.tsx: custom context + state

Critical: sidebar.tsx is the most complex UI component (14 forwardRef usages, custom context). Reference solid-ui.com sidebar and the previous attempt.
acceptance_criteria:
- All ui/ components are Solid-compatible
- Kobalte-based components render correctly
- Resizable panels work via @corvu/resizable
- Command palette works via cmdk-solid
- No Radix UI imports remain
- No forwardRef usage (use props.ref instead)
notes:
solid-ui.com uses copy-paste model like shadcn — you own the component code.
The sidebar component needs the most attention (14 forwardRef usages).
Consider running solidui-cli for bulk generation, then adjust imports.

title: Convert icon system to Solid
description: Convert the icon runtime and lucide icon re-exports from React to Solid.
design:
src/renderer/components/icons/runtime.tsx:
- Drop forwardRef wrapper, use plain component with props.ref
- unplugin-icons with compiler: jsx, jsx: solid generates Solid-compatible components
- The withIcon wrapper simplifies to a plain function that spreads props

src/renderer/components/icons/lucide.tsx:
- Swap lucide-react imports to lucide-solid (~80 icon re-exports)
- Once runtime.tsx is converted, lucide.tsx just needs import path changes

File icon components (fa.tsx, si.tsx, di.tsx, vsc.tsx) are simple SVG wrappers.
acceptance_criteria:
- Icons render in Solid components
- No lucide-react or forwardRef imports
- unplugin-icons generates Solid-compatible output
notes: Small task, can be done alongside UI components.

title: Convert views and core layout components
description: Convert the top-level views and layout components that form the app's main content areas.
design:
Files:
- views/Workspace.tsx (~400+ lines, heavy hook usage — largest component)
- views/Welcome.tsx
- components/MainContent.tsx
- components/MainContentArea.tsx
- components/ErrorBoundary.tsx → use Solid built-in <ErrorBoundary> component
- components/SettingsPage.tsx (47 hooks to convert)

Translation rules:
- className → class
- {condition && <X/>} → <Show when={condition}><X/></Show>
- .map() → <For each={list}>{(item) => ...}</For>
- Never destructure props
- React.FC<Props> → Component<Props>
- All useState/useEffect/useMemo/useCallback conversions
acceptance_criteria:
- Workspace view renders with all panels
- Welcome screen displays on first launch
- Settings page renders all tabs
- ErrorBoundary catches errors gracefully
- No React imports in any of these files
notes: Workspace.tsx is the most complex — uses nearly every hook and UI component. If this works, the app works.

title: Convert chat and terminal components
description: Convert chat interface and terminal emulator components from React to Solid.
design:
Files:
- ChatInterface.tsx (70+ hooks — second most complex component)
- ChatTabs.tsx
- CreateChatModal.tsx
- ConversationTabButton.tsx
- TerminalPane.tsx (uses forwardRef + useImperativeHandle → convert to ref callback)
- TerminalContextFooter.tsx
- TerminalSearchOverlay.tsx
- ExpandedTerminalModal.tsx (uses createPortal → Solid Portal)
- LifecycleTerminalView.tsx

Terminal components wrap xterm which is framework-agnostic DOM.
Convert useRef + useEffect lifecycle to let ref + onMount/onCleanup.
forwardRef + useImperativeHandle → expose methods via props.ref callback.
acceptance_criteria:
- Chat interface renders and accepts input
- Terminal renders and accepts commands
- Terminal search overlay works
- Expanded terminal modal opens via Portal
- No React imports
notes: xterm is purely imperative DOM — the Solid wrapper is thin.

title: Add more color schemes
close_reason: User decided to remove this task

title: Port this build over to solid-js from react
description:
## Scope
Port the renderer from React to Solid while preserving existing Electron behavior, workspace flows, and information architecture.

This epic covers:
- renderer boot and provider composition
- router and navigation preservation
- local UI primitives and higher-level component surfaces
- hook and context migration
- deletion of the in-app browser preview instead of porting it
- final verification, test migration, and React cleanup

## Design
- Keep `src/main/**`, preload IPC ownership, and Electron process boundaries intact unless a child task explicitly removes dead preview code.
- Preserve current product behavior and route structure rather than redesigning the UI during the framework migration.
- Prefer Solid equivalents for libraries the app already uses conceptually, especially TanStack router/query patterns, instead of swapping paradigms mid-migration.
- Break work into implementation-sized tasks that map to real renderer boundaries so later coding agents can execute them without re-scoping.

## Success Criteria
- The app boots and major workspace flows still work under Solid.
- The renderer no longer depends on React runtime packages.
- The in-app browser preview surface is removed and replaced with existing external-browser flows.
- Build, type-check, tests, and a smoke pass complete after the migration slices land.
notes: Blocked on emdash-onx.10. This cannot be represented as a blocks dependency because emdash-onx.10 is already a child of the epic, which would create a cycle.

title: Remove in-app browser preview
description:
## Scope
Remove the embedded browser preview surface instead of porting it to Solid.

Touch the browser-preview stack end to end:
- renderer files such as `src/renderer/components/BrowserPane.tsx`, `src/renderer/components/titlebar/BrowserToggleButton.tsx`, `src/renderer/components/BrowserPreviewSettingsCard.tsx`, `src/renderer/views/Workspace.tsx`, and `src/renderer/providers/BrowserProvider.tsx`
- preload/browser IPC in `src/main/preload.ts` and `src/main/ipc/browserIpc.ts`
- main-process service and window configuration in `src/main/services/browserViewService.ts` and `src/main/app/window.ts`
- preview settings and preview-specific telemetry in `src/main/settings.ts`, `src/main/telemetry.ts`, and related tests/types

## Design
- Replace any remaining preview/open actions with the existing `openExternal` path or host-preview-only behavior where appropriate.
- Delete preview-specific APIs rather than leaving deprecated wrappers behind.
- Remove `webviewTag` support from the main window once the renderer no longer embeds the preview surface.
- Update TypeScript declarations and tests alongside the removal so later migration tasks do not need preview compatibility shims.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No renderer path mounts `BrowserPane`, `BrowserProvider`, or the browser preview toggle/settings UI.
- Preload and main process no longer expose or handle `browser:view:*` APIs.
- The window configuration no longer enables the webview tag for the removed preview feature.
- Link-opening and localhost-preview flows still have an intentional behavior after the removal.

title: Primitives: forms, inputs, and small content controls
description:
## Scope
Port the form, input, status, and small content primitives under `src/renderer/components/ui` that are broadly reused across the app.

Start with files such as:
- `button.tsx`
- `input.tsx`
- `textarea.tsx`
- `checkbox.tsx`
- `radio-group.tsx`
- `select.tsx`
- `label.tsx`
- `badge.tsx`
- `card.tsx`
- `progress.tsx`
- `separator.tsx`
- `spinner.tsx`
- `shortcut-hint.tsx`
- `slug-input.tsx`
- `relative-time.tsx`
- `pr-status-skeleton.tsx`

## Design
- Preserve local exports and styling conventions so app surfaces can keep importing from `@/components/ui/*`.
- Replace React-only refs/events/composition patterns with Solid equivalents without widening the API.
- Keep this task focused on reusable leaf primitives rather than app-specific feature components.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted primitive files compile and run without React imports.
- Their public props and import paths remain stable enough for downstream component migration tasks.

title: Primitives: overlays, layout, and shared wrappers
description:
## Scope
Port the overlay, disclosure, navigation, and shell-oriented primitives under `src/renderer/components/ui`.

Start with files such as:
- `accordion.tsx`
- `alert-dialog.tsx`
- `alert.tsx`
- `breadcrumb.tsx`
- `collapsible.tsx`
- `context-menu.tsx`
- `dialog.tsx`
- `markdown-renderer.tsx`
- `popover.tsx`
- `scroll-area.tsx`
- `resizable.tsx`
- `sidebar.tsx`
- `right-sidebar.tsx`
- `toast.tsx`
- `toaster.tsx`
- `tooltip.tsx`

## Design
- Preserve the app-owned wrapper layer so higher-level component tasks are insulated from package churn.
- Keep accessibility and keyboard behavior on parity with the current wrappers.
- Treat `markdown-renderer` and shell/sidebar wrappers as part of the shared UI surface because many app features depend on them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted overlay/layout primitives no longer depend on React.
- App surfaces can continue importing them from the existing local module paths.
- Overlay/sidebar behavior remains compatible with current keyboard and layout flows.

title: Hooks: app-state and workspace management
description:
## Scope
Migrate the workspace/app-state hook layer to Solid while preserving the contracts consumed by providers and shell components.

Likely files include:
- `useLocalStorage.ts`
- `useTheme.ts`
- `useFeatureFlag.ts`
- `usePanelLayout.ts`
- `useWorkspaceConnection.ts`
- `useProjectManagement.tsx`
- `useTaskManagement.ts`
- `useTaskSettings.ts`
- `useTaskStatus.ts`
- `useTaskUnread.ts`
- `useStatusUnread.ts`
- `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`
- `useDeleteRisks.ts`

Router-specific navigation hooks belong in `emdash-onx.7`, not here.

## Design
- Keep return shapes and state semantics stable so provider/component migration can happen incrementally.
- Replace React memo/effect/ref patterns with Solid primitives only where the hook contract requires them.
- Avoid mixing PR/file/terminal domains into this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted app-state hooks no longer depend on React hook APIs.
- Existing provider/component consumers can migrate without inventing new state contracts.

title: Hooks: file, git, review, and PR workflows
description:
## Scope
Migrate the file, git, review, and pull-request hook layer to Solid.

Likely files include:
- `useFileIndex.ts`
- `useFileManager.ts`
- `useFileDiff.ts`
- `useFileChanges.ts`
- `useTaskChanges.ts`
- `usePullRequests.ts`
- `useCreatePR.tsx`
- `useCheckRuns.ts`
- `useAutoCheckRunsRefresh.ts`
- `useAutoPrRefresh.ts`
- `usePrComments.ts`
- `usePrStatus.ts`
- `useLineComments.ts`
- `useDiffEditorComments.ts`
- `useEditorDiffDecorations.ts`
- `useOpenInApps.ts`

## Design
- Preserve async loading/error semantics expected by file/diff/review components.
- Keep network and electron-API integration logic inside the existing hook boundaries when those boundaries still make sense.
- Do not absorb terminal, automation, or project/workspace state hooks into this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted git/file/review hooks compile without React hooks.
- Their public return contracts remain compatible with the existing component layer.

title: Hooks: terminal, agent, automation, and remote workflows
description:
## Scope
Migrate the terminal, agent, automation, and remote/integration hook layer to Solid.

Likely files include:
- `useAgentEvents.ts`
- `useAgentPreference.ts`
- `useTaskAgentNames.ts`
- `useAutomationTrigger.ts`
- `useChangelogNotification.ts`
- `useUpdateNotifier.tsx`
- `useUpdater.ts`
- `useRemoteProject.ts`
- `useSshConnections.ts`
- `useTerminalSearch.ts`
- `useTerminalSelection.ts`
- `useTerminalViewportWheelForwarding.ts`
- `useContentSearch.ts`
- `useInitialPromptInjection.ts`
- `useCommentInjection.ts`
- `useCliAgentDetection.ts`
- `useTelemetryConsent.ts`
- `useConversationStatus.ts`
- `useAutoScrollOnTaskSwitch.ts`
- `useIsMonochrome.ts`

## Design
- Preserve event-subscription and cleanup behavior carefully; many of these hooks bridge renderer state with terminals, IPC, or remote connections.
- Keep this slice focused on non-router, non-PR, non-core-app-state hooks.
- Favor stable contracts for downstream terminal/chat/integration components.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted terminal/agent/integration hooks no longer rely on React hook APIs.
- Subscription cleanup and event lifecycles remain explicit and Solid-compatible.

title: Components: shell, navigation, and settings surfaces
description:
## Scope
Port the shell, navigation, and settings surfaces that define the overall application frame.

Own components such as:
- `src/renderer/views/Workspace.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/titlebar/**`
- `src/renderer/components/sidebar/**`
- `src/renderer/components/MainContent.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/MainContentArea.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/HomeView.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/SettingsPage.tsx`
- settings cards and shared shell helpers such as `CommandPalette*`, `ExternalLinkModal`, `ModalRenderer`, and `ThemeProvider`

Browser preview UI owned by `emdash-onx.10` is removed, not ported here.

## Design
- Preserve the current workspace frame, titlebar/sidebar behavior, and settings-navigation flow.
- Use migrated primitive/provider/router layers rather than introducing local compatibility wrappers in these shell components.
- Keep this task focused on the application frame, not task-detail or editor/diff surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The shell/navigation/settings surface compiles without React.
- Workspace frame behavior remains compatible with current sidebar, titlebar, command palette, and settings flows.
- Browser preview UI is absent rather than silently reintroduced.

title: Components: task and project management surfaces
description:
## Scope
Port the task/project/workspace management surfaces that drive the core product workflows.

Own components such as:
- `ProjectMainView.tsx`
- `TaskList.tsx`, `TaskItem.tsx`, `TaskModal.tsx`
- `TaskSettings*`, `TaskChanges.tsx`, `TaskHoverActionCard.tsx`
- `BranchSelect.tsx`, `BaseBranchControls.tsx`
- `OpenPrsSection.tsx`, `MergePrSection.tsx`, `FileChangesPanel.tsx`
- project/task CRUD and move dialogs
- project/workspace cards tightly coupled to task/project management

## Design
- Preserve current task/project workflow behavior, selection state, and bulk-action semantics.
- Build on the migrated primitives, provider layer, and git/file/review hooks instead of re-owning that logic in components.
- Exclude chat/terminal/editor/diff specialty surfaces from this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The core task/project management surface compiles without React.
- Task/project flows still operate through the existing hooks/providers and maintain the current UX semantics.

title: Components: chat, terminal, editor, and integration surfaces
description:
## Scope
Port the specialized feature surfaces that sit on top of the shared shell and state layers.

Own components/directories such as:
- `ChatInterface.tsx`, `ChatTabs.tsx`, related review/chat helpers
- terminal surfaces including `TerminalPane.tsx`, `TaskTerminalPanel.tsx`, `LifecycleTerminalView.tsx`
- `diff-viewer/**`
- `FileExplorer/**`
- `monaco/**`
- `kanban/**`
- `automations/**`
- `ssh/**`
- `mcp/**` and `skills/**`
- issue selectors/tooltips, integration setup forms/cards, and other specialized feature components not covered by `emdash-onx.16` or `emdash-onx.17`

`BrowserPane.tsx` is deleted in `emdash-onx.10` instead of being ported here.

## Design
- Keep this task bounded to specialized renderer surfaces whose complexity comes from terminals, editors, integrations, or rich feature workflows.
- Depend on the migrated primitive, hook, provider, router, and browser-preview-removal work instead of carrying fallback React shims.
- Preserve existing feature behavior before optimizing or redesigning any surface.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The specialized renderer surfaces compile without React.
- Browser preview components are removed rather than migrated.
- Terminal/editor/integration surfaces continue to use the existing domain hooks and IPC boundaries after the framework swap.

title: Remove in-app feedback and related telemetry
description: Remove the in-app feedback modal, its titlebar trigger, the associated keyboard shortcut (Cmd+Shift+F), and the feedback telemetry event. The feature sends feedback to a Discord webhook and is not being carried forward into the Solid migration — strip it out on the React side so migration tasks start from a smaller surface.
design:
## Files to change

1. **Delete** `src/renderer/components/FeedbackModal.tsx` (382-line React component with Discord webhook, image attachment, toast feedback).

2. **Edit** `src/renderer/components/titlebar/Titlebar.tsx`:
   - Remove `FeedbackModal` import (line 17).
   - Remove `isFeedbackOpen` state, `feedbackButtonRef` ref, `handleOpenFeedback`/`handleCloseFeedback` callbacks.
   - Remove `useEffect` that dispatches `emdash:overlay:changed` for feedback modal state (~lines 147-152).
   - Remove `useEffect` that registers the Cmd+Shift+F global shortcut (~lines 154-184).
   - Remove the feedback toolbar button JSX (~lines 302-328) and the `<FeedbackModal />` render (~lines 363-367).
   - The `useRef` import may become unused — verify and clean up.

3. **Edit** `src/main/telemetry.ts`: remove `'toolbar_feedback_clicked'` from the `TelemetryEvent` union type (line 65).

4. **Edit** `src/main/ipc/telemetryIpc.ts`: remove `'toolbar_feedback_clicked'` from the `RENDERER_ALLOWED_EVENTS` Set (line 61).

## Scope boundaries

- The `emdash:overlay:changed` custom event is also dispatched by `ModalProvider.tsx` — do NOT remove the event listener infrastructure, only the feedback-specific dispatch.
- `WorkspaceProviderInfoCard.tsx` shares the same Discord webhook URL but is a separate feature (workspace provider access requests). Leave it alone unless a separate task addresses it.
- Icons used by the feedback modal (`MessageSquare`, `CornerDownLeft`, `Paperclip`) are reused elsewhere — no icon cleanup needed.
- The shortcut is inline in Titlebar, not in `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts` or `settings.ts`, so no settings migration is needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- FeedbackModal.tsx no longer exists in the tree.
- Titlebar renders without a feedback button; no feedback-related state, refs, callbacks, or effects remain.
- Cmd+Shift+F no longer opens a feedback modal.
- The telemetry event type 'toolbar_feedback_clicked' is absent from both the type union and the allowed-events set.
- The app builds, type-checks, and boots without errors.
- No other features are broken (overlay events, workspace provider card, toolbar layout).
notes: The feedback modal posts to a Discord webhook (hardcoded URL in FeedbackModal.tsx). No server-side cleanup is needed — just remove the client code. After deletion, grep for 'FeedbackModal' and 'toolbar_feedback_clicked' to confirm no dangling references remain. Run `bun run build` and `bun run typecheck` (or equivalent) to verify.

title: Build & dependency swap
description:
## Scope
Replace the React-specific renderer toolchain and package set with Solid equivalents without changing the Electron main-process build.

Touch:
- `package.json` renderer dependencies and devDependencies
- `vite.config.ts`
- `tsconfig.json`
- renderer test harness dependencies and JSX/runtime configuration

## Design
- Swap the renderer to a Solid JSX runtime and Solid Vite plugin.
- Preserve the existing alias structure, renderer root, and main-process TypeScript builds.
- Migrate TanStack integrations to the Solid packages instead of replacing router/query architecture during the same task.
- Keep this task focused on package/config readiness; do not start porting feature code here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A clean install succeeds with the Solid renderer dependency set.
- Renderer `.tsx` files compile through the Solid JSX runtime in dev, build, and test config.
- React runtime packages are removed from the intended end state or clearly isolated as temporary follow-up items.
- Downstream migration tasks can import Solid packages without additional config work.

title: Entry point & foundation
description:
## Scope
Port the renderer boot path and shared foundation so later feature slices have a stable Solid entry point.

Touch the renderer shell files that currently define application startup and provider composition:
- `src/renderer/main.tsx`
- `src/renderer/App.tsx`
- `src/renderer/AppShell.tsx`
- any JSX-runtime-sensitive icon/bootstrap helpers touched by those files

## Design
- Replace `ReactDOM.createRoot(...).render(...)` with the Solid render entry point.
- Rebuild the top-level app shell so provider ordering and first-launch/workspace routing behavior remain intact.
- Keep the boundary between framework-agnostic utilities and renderer-framework code explicit so child tasks know where Solid should stop.
- Do not absorb router migration, provider internals, or feature-surface porting into this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The renderer boots through a Solid entry point.
- The top-level shell still routes first-launch users to the welcome flow and everyone else to the workspace flow.
- Child tasks have a stable Solid app shell to build against without redefining startup behavior.

title: UI primitives migration
description:
## Scope
Replace the local React-based UI primitives in `src/renderer/components/ui` with Solid-compatible implementations while keeping the app-owned import surface stable for downstream component tasks.

This epic is split so later agents can port primitives in coherent families instead of editing the full `components/ui` tree in one pass.

## Design
- Preserve local component names and import paths whenever possible.
- Port primitive families first, then let higher-level app surfaces migrate against those local wrappers.
- Keep styling conventions (`cn`, class-variance-authority patterns, Tailwind classes) aligned with the current repo instead of redesigning primitives.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks cover the full primitive surface under `src/renderer/components/ui`.
- App-level component tasks can migrate against local Solid primitives rather than importing framework packages directly.

title: Hook migration
description:
## Scope
Replace React hook implementations in `src/renderer/hooks` with Solid primitives while preserving the consumer-facing contracts that the component layer already uses.

This epic is split by domain so later agents can migrate hooks without reopening scope for unrelated feature areas.

## Design
- Preserve hook/module boundaries where they are still useful to the app.
- Convert state/effect/subscription logic to Solid primitives without redesigning product behavior.
- Keep router-specific hook work with the router migration task instead of scattering navigation changes across unrelated hook tasks.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks cover the current renderer hook surface in domain-sized slices.
- Downstream component tasks can adopt Solid-backed hooks without inventing new state APIs.

title: Context providers migration
description:
## Scope
Port the renderer context/provider layer to Solid while preserving the existing provider ordering and public context hooks.

Primary files:
- `src/renderer/contexts/AppContextProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/AppSettingsProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/EmdashAccountProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/ExternalLinkContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/GitWorkspaceBusyContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/GithubContextProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/KeyboardSettingsContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/ModalProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/ProjectManagementProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/TaskManagementContext.tsx`

`BrowserProvider` is intentionally removed in `emdash-onx.10` instead of being ported here.

## Design
- Preserve the imperative APIs exposed by providers such as modal management and settings updates.
- Keep provider composition compatible with `AppShell` after the Solid entry-point task lands.
- Port context consumers to Solid-compatible hooks/signals inside the provider modules; do not absorb unrelated component work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The listed provider/context files no longer depend on React context or React hooks.
- Provider order and externally consumed context APIs remain compatible with the rest of the renderer.
- Browser preview provider code is not reintroduced as part of this task.

title: Router and navigation migration
description:
## Scope
Migrate routing and route-aware navigation helpers to Solid while preserving the current hash-based workspace URLs and overlay flows.

Primary files:
- `src/renderer/App.tsx`
- `src/renderer/router.tsx`
- `src/renderer/hooks/useWorkspaceRouter.ts`
- any tightly-coupled route helper usage needed to keep `workspaceRoutes` behavior intact

## Design
- Preserve the existing route inventory and hash-history navigation model.
- Keep the TanStack router approach already present in the app, but switch to the Solid package surface.
- Ensure route-driven overlays such as settings and diff views still open/close via URL state rather than local modal-only state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The app exposes the same route shapes and route params after the migration.
- Workspace navigation helpers still generate and consume the existing href/search patterns.
- Route-driven overlay flows continue to round-trip through the URL correctly.

title: Application surfaces migration
description:
## Scope
Port renderer components outside the primitive layer in vertical slices after foundations land.

This epic deliberately excludes `components/ui/**` and excludes the in-app browser preview, which is removed in `emdash-onx.10`.

## Design
- Migrate app surfaces in slices that match real feature ownership instead of attempting a single pass across ~200 component files.
- Keep current product behavior, layout, and module boundaries unless a child task has a repo-grounded reason to adjust them.
- Let each child task name the feature surfaces it owns so later agents can work without re-triaging the whole component tree.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks cover the app-specific component surface in implementation-sized slices.
- The remaining React-dependent component work is discoverable by child issue rather than hidden in one catch-all task.

title: Validation, test migration, and React removal
description:
## Scope
Finish the migration by updating the test harness, removing remaining React-only leftovers, and running full verification.

Touch:
- renderer/component test utilities and imports under `src/test/**`
- any renderer config or helper files still carrying React-specific compatibility code
- final search-and-destroy for stale React imports after the migration slices land

## Design
- Replace React-only component test setup with Solid-compatible testing utilities.
- Run broad repo checks only after the leaf migration tasks are complete so failures point to real regressions instead of half-migrated code.
- Treat this task as cleanup/verification, not a dumping ground for unfinished feature migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bun type-check`, `bun test`, and `bun run build` pass for the migrated codebase.
- Remaining React imports/runtime deps are removed from renderer code and tests, or any intentional exception is documented explicitly.
- A smoke pass covers the primary workspace flow after the Solid migration lands.

title: Fix authentication bug
description: Login fails with special characters in password
